title: strychnos barteri
intro: Strychnos barteri, also known as the West African strychnine tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of West Africa. It is a medicinal plant used for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Strychnos barteri have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are used in traditional medicine in West Africa, with some studies suggesting potential applications in modern medicine.
